title: Smoky Roasted Mediterranean Celeriac Tagine
description: A sour, cheese-pull, crowd-pleaser Mediterranean dish featuring celeriac, tagine-cooked to perfection. This macrobiotic-friendly recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Peel and chop the celeriac into 1-inch cubes.
In a large bowl, whisk together the olive oil, cumin, coriander, paprika, smoked paprika, chili flakes, salt, and black pepper.
Add the celeriac to the bowl and toss to coat with the spice mixture.
Transfer the celeriac to a 9x13 inch baking dish and roast for 30 minutes.
Add the chopped onion and minced garlic to the baking dish and continue roasting for an additional 20 minutes.
Stir in the diced tomatoes, chopped kalamata olives, and crumbled feta cheese.
Cover the dish with foil and continue roasting for 10 minutes.
Remove the foil and continue roasting for an additional 10 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Sprinkle the chopped parsley and cilantro over the top of the tagine and serve hot garnished with a squeeze of lemon juice.
Let the tagine rest for 10 minutes before serving.
Serve hot, garnished with additional parsley and cilantro if desired.
Refrigerate or freeze leftovers for up to 3 days.
Reheat the tagine in the oven or on the stovetop before serving.
